topic: How Abyssal Surge turns 3D source assets into a playable dark-fantasy battlefield
music: dark ritual percussion and low strings, instrumental, no vocals

beats.scene: Raw 3D source renders arrive as isolated paper specimens before becoming game-readable pieces.
beats.narration: These are only source assets: a gate, a citadel, a sovereign. They do not make a battlefield—yet.

shots.scene: A torn contact sheet of the Gate Sovereign, Veil Citadel, and source material swatches, isolated on a void-black paper field.
shots.element_motion: The three specimen cards settle at staggered depths, violet registration marks pulse, tape corners flutter, and tiny paper shards scatter once while all lettering remains fixed.

shots.scene: A close paper strip tracks from a 3D idle render to a texture swatch and a blank tactical grid stamped NOT YET PLAYABLE.
shots.element_motion: Texture scraps slide under the specimen, a rigid paper arrow travels left-to-right once, halftone dots pulse, and the final red stamp settles without moving the text.

beats.scene: Source art is decomposed into readable silhouettes, action atlases, and landmarks.
beats.narration: Each render is cut into readable silhouettes, atlases, actions, and tactical landmarks.

shots.scene: A workshop-board collage separates a sovereign render, unit atlas, action icon, and citadel landmark into four labeled paper layers.
shots.element_motion: The atlas rows offset in shallow parallax, scissors marks trace around the sovereign, action tabs hinge open, and violet ink misregistration breathes gently.

shots.scene: A vertical inspection of silhouettes and atlas cells, ending on a clean cut-out pinned above a tactical tile.
shots.element_motion: Atlas cells tick downward like a contact sheet, the clean silhouette pops into focus, tape edges lift, and two paper labels settle into alignment.

beats.bg: bruise purple with ember-orange signal marks
beats.scene: Capture, possession, and domain rules connect the visual pieces into player decisions.
beats.narration: Then capture, possession, and domain rules connect those pieces into decisions the player can see.

shots.scene: A tactical board reveals three torn action cards—CAPTURE, POSSESS, DOMAIN—wired by paper arrows into a battlefield map.
shots.element_motion: Action cards slap down in sequence, rigid arrows extend between them, command pips flicker, and a single violet signal ribbon crosses the board then exits.

shots.scene: A close cut-in on a capture icon transforming a neutral node into a violet-controlled landmark without changing the underlying art.
shots.element_motion: One capture seal slides into the node, control rings pulse outward, two unit cut-outs pivot toward the objective, and scraps settle on the beat.

beats.bg: starless teal-black opening into sovereign violet
beats.scene: The assembled dark-fantasy battlefield now answers player movement with readable tactical consequences.
beats.narration: Now the same dark-fantasy art answers every move. Source assets become a playable Abyssal Surge battlefield.

shots.scene: The finished battlefield collage combines the portal, units, capture ring, and dark-fantasy skyline into one playable tableau.
shots.element_motion: Units advance in short paper steps, the capture ring radiates, portal fragments orbit as rigid scraps, and embers drift behind the stable headline.

shots.scene: A locked-off final battlefield with the bilingual film title and a hard-cut-ready Abyssal Surge seal.
shots.element_motion: Only low ember flicker, subtle paper breathing, and a final seal pulse; camera and bilingual title remain perfectly locked for the payoff.
